question.stem: Order and compare numbers beyond 1000; use <, > and = signs; Count forwards and backwards with positive and negative whole numbers, including through zero. | Learning Journey | Subtopics: | N1.1 to N1.4 | ↓ | Integer place value: | words and figures, positioning on a number line and comparing and ordering | Subtopics: | N1.9 to N1.12 | ↓ | Rounding: | number lines and rounding to the | nearest 10, 100, 1000, integer and to decimal places and significant figures | Subtopic: | N1.5 | ↓ | Midpoint and median | Subtopics: | N1.13 to N1.15 | ↓ | Negative place value: | positioning on a number line, comparing and ordering | Subtopics: | N1.6 to N1.8 | ↗ | Decimal place value: | positioning on a number line, comparing and ordering | Key knowledge | By the end of this topic you should know… | The place value of integers, decimals and negatives; The median of a list of numbers is the middle value when the numbers are ordered; Very large or very small numbers can be written in standard form to make them easier to read. | By the end of this topic should be able to do… | Position, compare and order | integers | , | decimals | and | negatives | ; | Round | numbers to any given degree of accuracy; Calculate the | median | from a list of numbers; Write numbers in | standard form
